title: Roche Expands AI Capabilities with 2,100+ Nvidia Chips to Accelerate Drug Development
summary: Berlin: Swiss drugmaker Roche said on Monday it had expanded its artificial intelligence computing capacity with more than 2,100 Nvidia chips to support drug and diagnostics development.Roche said the...

contentHtml:
Berlin: Swiss drugmaker Roche said on Monday it had expanded its artificial intelligence computing capacity with more than 2,100 Nvidia chips to support drug and diagnostics development.

Roche said the additional hardware ÔÇîwould speed Ôüáup Ôüáwork across its research and development operations, including ÔÇőmodelling, data analysis and clinical trial processes.

The drugmaker said ÔÇőit had deployed 2,176 Nvidia Blackwell graphics processing units (GPUs) across sites in the U.S. ÔÇőand Europe, giving it the Ôüálargest GPU ÔÇîfootprint in the industry.

The build-up, ÔÇőwhich ÔÇőbegan in 2023, is part of Ôüáa wider collaboration with Nvidia by ÔÇőRoche, which has been increasing investment ÔÇőin AI tools as large pharmaceutical groups compete to cut development timelines and reduce costs.

"In healthcare, time is the most critical variable," Chief Digital and Technology Officer ÔÇîWafaa Mamilli said.

Drugmakers have announced a slew of deals for tools to ÔÇőunleash the ÔÇőpromise of Ôüáartificial intelligence, seen as the biggest technological breakthrough since the internet.

Agentic AI, which requires little human ÔÇőintervention, could increase clinical development productivity by about 35% to 45% over the next five years, consultancy McKinsey said last year.

Source : Reuters
